description.en: An integrated women empowerment umbrella scheme by Ministry of WCD containing Sambal (safety) and Samarthya (empowerment) verticals, covering One Stop Centres, WHL, and PMMVY.

benefits.en: Welfare services, emergency helpline response, legal/medical aid, and maternity cash incentives up to ₹6,000.

en: Step 1: Contact national helpline 181 or visit nearest One Stop Centre.
Step 2: Submit details of requirement/maternity case.
Step 3: Access shelter, clinical aid, or DBT cash benefits directly.
Step 1: Contact national helpline 181 or visit nearest One Stop Centre.
Step 2: Submit details of requirement/maternity case.
Step 3: Access shelter, clinical aid, or DBT cash benefits directly.

description.en: Provides safe, secure, and conveniently located hostel accommodation for working women, along with daycare facilities for their children where possible.

benefits.en: Affordable rent (capped at 15% of salary for single room), stay up to 3-5 years, and daycare center access for children.

en: Step 1: Locate an approved Working Women Hostel in your city.
Step 2: Submit admission application form to the hostel manager.
Step 3: Provide employment certificate and salary certificate for rent calculations.
Step 1: Locate an approved Working Women Hostel in your city.
Step 2: Submit admission application form to the hostel manager.
Step 3: Provide employment certificate and salary certificate for rent calculations.

description.en: Livelihood and sports support initiative for women athletes and coaches in India, providing financial impetus and grassroot sports leagues.

benefits.en: Financial support, specialized coaching, training resources, and sports equipment support for elite women athletes.

en: Step 1: Visit the portal of Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Step 2: Submit details of your sports accomplishments and achievements.
Step 3: Apply for training support or elite athlete allowance.
Step 1: Visit the portal of Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Step 2: Submit details of your sports accomplishments and achievements.
Step 3: Apply for training support or elite athlete allowance.

title.hi: खेलो इंडिया राष्ट्रीय खेल विकास कार्यक्रम

description.en: A national programme to promote sports culture, establish KISCE training centers, and identify young talent to provide scholarships.

benefits.en: Scholarship and funding of ₹6.28 Lakh per annum per athlete (including ₹10,000/month out-of-pocket allowance) and training in accredited academies.

en: Step 1: Register on the Khelo India Portal.
Step 2: Upload sports achievements and create profile.
Step 3: Shortlisted athletes undergo fitness and talent search trials.
Step 1: Register on the Khelo India Portal.
Step 2: Upload sports achievements and create profile.
Step 3: Shortlisted athletes undergo fitness and talent search trials.

description.en: Achieving Sports Milestone by Inspiring Women Through Action (ASMITA) Leagues promotes sports participation and competitive exposure among Indian women athletes.

benefits.en: Access to national women's sports leagues, specialized training, and business loan support via SBI Asmita.

documentsRequired: Sports achievement details / Business registration documents

en: Step 1: Visit the official MyGov campaign link or Stand-Up India/SBI portal.
Step 2: Fill application form and upload sports credentials.
Step 3: Apply for financial or coaching support.
Step 1: Visit the official MyGov campaign link or Stand-Up India/SBI portal.
Step 2: Fill application form and upload sports credentials.
Step 3: Apply for financial or coaching support.

description.en: Organizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides revolving and capitalization funds to access financial services and sustainable livelihoods.

benefits.en: Capacity building, revolving funds (up to ₹30,000), Community Investment Funds (up to ₹2.5 Lakh), bank linkage, and interest subventions.

en: Step 1: Register on the Aajeevika NRLM portal.
Step 2: Create a Self Help Group profile with bank account details.
Step 3: Access revolving funds and capitalization support directly.
Step 1: Register on the Aajeevika NRLM portal.
Step 2: Create a Self Help Group profile with bank account details.
Step 3: Access revolving funds and capitalization support directly.

description.en: Small informal groups of 10 to 20 poor women who save regularly and lend internally to meet emergency needs, supported by NABARD.

benefits.en: Access to bank accounts, interest-earning savings, internal credit, and leadership capacity building.

en: Step 1: Contact local animator or NGO to form a group of 10-20 women.
Step 2: Set up savings accounts at the nearest commercial or cooperative bank.
Step 3: Begin regular savings and meetings.
Step 1: Contact local animator or NGO to form a group of 10-20 women.
Step 2: Set up savings accounts at the nearest commercial or cooperative bank.
Step 3: Begin regular savings and meetings.

description.en: A sub-component of DAY-NRLM to empower women in agriculture by making systematic investments to enhance their participation and sustainable livelihoods.

benefits.en: Skill training in sustainable agriculture, distribution of gender-friendly tools, capacity building, and market linkages.

en: Step 1: Register through your local SHG federation.
Step 2: Submit details of your agricultural activities.
Step 3: Access training and tools distribution programs.
Step 1: Register through your local SHG federation.
Step 2: Submit details of your agricultural activities.
Step 3: Access training and tools distribution programs.

description.en: Promotes menstrual hygiene among rural girls and women in Maharashtra by providing subsidized sanitary napkins through Self Help Groups.

benefits.en: Subsidized sanitary napkins distributed to school-going girls (₹5 pack) and rural women (subsidized cost), and hygiene awareness.

en: Step 1: SHGs register on the Asmita portal.
Step 2: Girls/women can buy subsidized packs from local SHGs or Anganwadis.
Step 1: SHGs register on the Asmita portal.
Step 2: Girls/women can buy subsidized packs from local SHGs or Anganwadis.

description.en: An initiative under DAY-NRLM to enable women in Self Help Groups (SHGs) to earn a sustainable annual household income above ₹1 Lakh through micro-enterprise support.

benefits.en: Capitalization support up to ₹20 Lakh per SHG, revolving funds, CIF support, low-interest bank loans, and marketing facilities (Saras centers).

en: Step 1: Join a local Self Help Group associated with NRLM.
Step 2: Engage in income-generating activities and apply at block level.
Step 3: Obtain marketing or micro-loan capitalization support.
Step 1: Join a local Self Help Group associated with NRLM.
Step 2: Engage in income-generating activities and apply at block level.
Step 3: Obtain marketing or micro-loan capitalization support.
